verses.text: Ahitofel a proposé une attaque surprise éclair contre David avec une petite armée (12 000 hommes, **17.1** ), ce qui ne laisserait pas le temps à David pour s'organiser et riposter. Ensuite, l'agent double de David, Houchaï, a suggéré de prendre plus de temps pour mobiliser toute l'armée d'Israël ( **17.11** ). Ce plan était censé leur donner un avantage numérique insurmontable et empêcherait David de s'engager dans des tactiques de guérilla. Parce que le Seigneur travaillait contre Absalom, il a rejeté la bonne stratégie d'Ahitofel et a accepté les mauvais conseils de Houchaï ( **17.14 ** ; cf. **1R 12.1-15** ).\n\n

Ahitofel a proposé une attaque surprise éclair contre David avec une petite armée (12 000 hommes, **17.1** ), ce qui ne laisserait pas le temps à David pour s'organiser et riposter. Ensuite, l'agent double de David, Houchaï, a suggéré de prendre plus de temps pour mobiliser toute l'armée d'Israël ( **17.11** ). Ce plan était censé leur donner un avantage numérique insurmontable et empêcherait David de s'engager dans des tactiques de guérilla. Parce que le Seigneur travaillait contre Absalom, il a rejeté la bonne stratégie d'Ahitofel et a accepté les mauvais conseils de Houchaï ( **17.14 ** ; cf. **1R 12.1-15** ).\n\n
Le texte hébreu montre que la loyauté de tout le peuple dépend de la mort de David. La version grecque dépeint une jeune épouse qui retourne auprès de son mari après une courte querelle.

17:4 And the saying pleased Absalom well, and all elders of Israel. That were of the privy council; for it was a well contrived scheme to surprise David his men by night, in the condition they were in; nothing like seizing an opportunity, and making quick dispatch in executing a villainous design; only it is strange that Absalom could so easily come into a scheme to take away the life of so indulgent a father, which is only to be accounted for by his ambition;

17:5 And when Hushai was come to Absalom,.... Into the council chamber, very probably: Absalom spake unto him, saying, Ahithophel hath spoken after this manner; and then related the counsel he had given as before: shall we do after his saying? is it right to proceed on this scheme, and carry it into execution? if not, speak thou; thy mind freely, without any reserve, or fear of giving any offence.

17:6 And Hushai said to Absalom,.... Having leave to give his opinion freely: the counsel that Ahithophel hath given is not good at this time; he owns that Ahithophel was a good counsellor, and that the counsel he gave was for the most part, if not always, good, but what he gave at this time was not so; the wisest of men may sometimes be mistaken. Abarbinel thinks he respects his former counsel, particularly that that was good, advising him to defile his father's bed, 2 Sam 16:21;

17:7 For (said Hushai) thou knowest thy father and his men, that they be mighty men,.... Men of courage and valour, and not such weak-handed and weak-hearted men, and so easily intimidated, as Ahithophel suggests: and they be chafed in their minds; or "bitter in soul" (f); not merely filled with trouble and anguish, and depressed in their spirits, on account of that, as the phrase sometimes signifies; but enraged and full of wrath at the rebellion raised against their prince, which obliged them with him to leave their habitations;

17:8 As a bear robbed of her whelps - All wild beasts are very furious when robbed of their young; but we have some remarkable instances of the maternal affection of the bear in such circumstances; see one at the end of the chapter.

17:9 And he also that is valiant, whose heart is as the heart of a lion, shall utterly melt,.... That is, whoever should hear of Absalom's forces, or a part of them, being routed, would be intimidated, though ever so courageous, or of such a lion-like temper and disposition; and even Ahithophel himself, notwithstanding all his boasted courage, his heart would fail, he would melt like water (h), Jos 7:5; should he meet with such a rebuff at first setting out.

17:10 Therefore I counsel,.... My advice is as follows: that all Israel be generally gathered unto thee, from Dan even to Beersheba, as the sand that is by the sea for multitude: not all the inhabitants of the land, but such as are fit to bear arms, or that were soldiers, employed in military affairs, in keeping garrisons, guarding the coasts, &c. even the militia of the nation, from the extreme boundary of it on the north to the extreme boundary of it on the south;
La conscription militaire nationale de Dan au nord d'Israël à Berchéva au sud d'Israël visait à constituer une force de frappe d'une supériorité écrasante.

17:12 we will light upon him as the dew falleth on the ground—No image could have symbolized the sudden onset of an enemy so graphically to an Oriental mind as the silent, irresistible, and rapid descent of this natural moisture on every field and blade of grass.

17:13 all Israel shall bring ropes to that city—In besieging a town, hooks or cranes were often thrown upon the walls or turrets, by which, with ropes attached to them, the besiegers, uniting all their force, pulled down the fortifications in a mass of ruins.

La stratégie d'Ahitofel était en fait meilleure. Cependant, Houchaï avait l'avantage psychologique et rhétorique, ainsi que la volonté du Seigneur de son côté, il a donc gagné la faveur d'Absalom. • Malgré les premières avancées, la prise de pouvoir d'Absalom était vouée à l'échec dès le départ.

17:15 Now therefore send quickly and tell David,.... What has been advised to on both sides: saying, lodge not this night in the plains of the wilderness; which reached to Jericho; this advice he gave, because he knew not whether his counsel would be abode by, though preferred; Absalom and the elders of Israel might change their minds, and Ahithophel might, by the strength of his oratory, prevail upon them after all to follow his advice; and therefore, to guard against the worst, he judged it most prudential in David to make all the haste he could from that place where he was, and not continue a night longer in it;

17:16 SECRET INTELLIGENCE SENT TO DAVID. (2 Sam 17:15-22) send quickly, and tell David—Apparently doubting that his advice would be followed, Hushai ordered secret intelligence to be conveyed to David of all that transpired, with an urgent recommendation to cross the Jordan without a moment's delay, lest Ahithophel's address and influence might produce a change on the prince's mind, and an immediate pursuit be determined on.
Voir **15.27** , **36** . • L'emplacement de la source des Blanchisseurs est incertain, mais elle ne devait pas se situer bien loin à l'est de Jérusalem.

Bahourim était la terre natale de Chiméi, le partisan benjaminite de Saül ( **16.5** ; **19.16** ). Les malédictions de Chiméi contre David à Bahourim reflétaient le déclin de David, tandis que la fuite d'Ahimaas et Yonatan représentait l'espoir du retour de David.

Bahourim était la terre natale de Chiméi, le partisan benjaminite de Saül ( **16.5** ; **19.16** ). Les malédictions de Chiméi contre David à Bahourim reflétaient le déclin de David, tandis que la fuite d'Ahimaas et Yonatan représentait l'espoir du retour de David.

Bahourim était la terre natale de Chiméi, le partisan benjaminite de Saül ( **16.5** ; **19.16** ). Les malédictions de Chiméi contre David à Bahourim reflétaient le déclin de David, tandis que la fuite d'Ahimaas et Yonatan représentait l'espoir du retour de David.

17:21 Then David arose, and all the people that were with him, and passed over Jordan,.... Taking the advice of Hushai: by the morning light there lacked not one of them that was not gone over Jordan; they travelled all night, and before it was morning they had all passed the river in boats, or through the fords, and not one was lost in the passage, nor any deserted, see John 17:12; and now perhaps David penned the forty second and forty third psalms, Ps 42:1.

17:22 And when Ahithophel saw that his counsel was not followed,.... But that of Hushai: he saddled his ass; or ordered it to be saddled: and arose, and gat him home to his house, to his city; which was Giloh in the tribe of Judah, 2 Sam 15:12, and put his household in order; made his will, and disposed of his estates, see Isa 38:1; or "commanded his house" or "household" (t); gave orders and instructions, both relating to himself when dead, where and how he should be buried, and to his family, how they should behave to one another and among their neighbours, and towards their superiors;
Ahitofel, qui prévoyait la victoire de David et la défaite d'Absalom, préféra se tuer par pendaison plutôt qu'être tué de la main de David.
Mahanaïm , autrefois la capitale d'Ichebaal et d'Abner ( **2.8** , **12** , **29** ), est devenue le centre stratégique de David pour ses opérations militaires.

Selon **1 Ch 2.15-16** , Abigaïl et Serouia étaient les sœurs de David. Cependant, Abigaïl étant appelée la fille de Nahach, elle et Serouia seraient les demi-sœurs de David (Nahach était probablement leur père ; sa veuve aurait ensuite épousé Jessé et donné naissance à David et ses frères).

17:26 Brought beds,.... For David and his men, who, fleeing from Jerusalem in haste, could bring none with them, and therefore were ill provided while in the plains of the wilderness; the Septuagint version says there were ten of them, and that they were of tapestry, wrought on both sides, and such the ancients used (z), see Pro 7:16; and so ten basins in the next clause: and basins, and earthen vessels; to put their food and liquors in, and eat and drink out of, and for other services: and wheat, and barley, and flour, and parched corn;

Chobi était probablement le frère de Hanoun, qui avait humilié les messagers de David après la mort de Nahach (chapitre **10 ;** voir note d'étude sur 10.1-5 ). • Makir était l'hôte de Mefibaal avant que David ne le convoque ( **9.4-5** ). • Barzillaï de Galaad n'était probablement pas le même que Barzillaï d'Abel-Mehola ( **21.8** ).

17:28 Brought beds - These no doubt consisted in skins of beasts, mats, carpets, and such like things. Basons - ספות sappoth. Probably wooden bowls, such as the Arabs still use to eat out of, and to knead their bread in. Earthen vessels - כלי יוצר keley yotser. Probably clay vessels, baked in the sun. These were perhaps used for lifting water, and boiling those articles which required to be cooked. Wheat, and barley, etc. - There is no direct mention of flesh-meat here;
17:29 in the wilderness—spread out beyond the cultivated tablelands into the steppes of Hauran. Next: 2 Samuel Chapter 18
